Job Description
As Senior Product Manager – Institutional, you will work directly with customers, sales, consulting, and operations to identify and validate common needs of the banks, larger institutional brokers, and non-bank market makers that make up our institutional target market.  You’ll translate those needs into actionable production direction in harmony with our overall strategy and collaborate closely with development colleagues to build new and enhanced capabilities to meet customer needs in differentiable ways.  Based in Singapore, Sydney, or Canberra, you'll follow your product enhancements into production to ensure they’re having the desired positive impact and gather new input for further advancement of our solutions.


Key Roles and Responsibilities

Product Strategy & Discovery

Partner with Sales, Relationship Management, and key clients to deeply understand institutional trading workflows and pain points

Work with the Managing Director – Institutional to size market opportunities, organize feature requests into cohesive initiatives, and prioritize within the institutional roadmap

Conduct customer research and discovery—including on-site visits with institutional clients in the APAC region

Keep tabs on competitive advancements, disseminate that knowledge throughout the team, and consider it in your roadmap refinement.

Define & Deliver

Define business requirements for institutional trading capabilities including:

Active Trader Aggregation UI for FX Spot and Swaps

Algorithmic Order Management

Advanced Systematic Hedging

Pre- and Post-trade Analytics and Execution Benchmarks

Collaborate with technical design and development teams to create specifications and architectural approaches

Identify proven 3rd party resources, frameworks, and tools to accelerate delivery when appropriate

Leverage AI to rapidly prototype features, generate early client feedback, and support technical design

Execute & Launch

Partner with the development team to plan releases, triage issues, and develop test plans

Work with operations to define deployment and support requirements

Design and manage pilot programs to drive customer adoption

Create sales enablement materials and conduct training sessions with Relationship Managers and Marketing

Support Sales in pitching new functionality to prospective customers as one means to training them to sell new capabilities.

Own the Outcomes

Be accountable for adoption and usage targets for initiatives in your product portfolio

Measure success through client adoption and recurring revenue generation




Minimum Requirements

7+ years of product management or development management experience in institutional FX trading technology

Direct experience in one or more of the following areas:

FX pricing and risk management

ECN or Active Trading Aggregator GUI/workflow design

EOMS or algorithmic execution

FX data and analytics

Deep understanding of institutional FX market structure and client segments (banks, hedge funds, institutional brokers)

Prior experience working on a bank trading desk OR as a product manager serving institutional trading clients

Experience working in distributed, global product teams




Nice to Have

Familiarity with oneZero's technology stack or similar trading platforms

Experience of AI tools for rapid prototyping of new functionality, and assisting with project management tasks

Job Title: Senior GIS Analyst (12 month Fixed Term) - NSW/QLD/VIC
Ad Text:
About Niche

Niche is the largest privately-owned specialist biodiversity and heritage consultancy in Australia. With a strong focus on delivering high-quality services and empowering our people to achieve sustainable outcomes, we work closely with clients across diverse industries to navigate their environmental and social challenges. These include important projects supporting Australia’s transition to clean, renewable energy, and delivering nature-positive, landscape-scale natural capital strategy. With a team of over 200 people, and growing, Niche isn’t only a great workplace, it’s a connected and inclusive community of incredible thinkers and doers, constantly innovating, learning and excelling at what they do.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Senior GIS Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will play a key part in delivering spatial and geospatial analysis services across a diverse range of projects.

You will be responsible for the collection, management, analysis, and visualisation of spatial data, ensuring the delivery of accurate and reliable geospatial information to support project planning, design, and decision-making. Working closely with ecology, heritage, and land restoration specialists, you will contribute to the delivery of high-quality, integrated spatial outputs for our clients.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead the technical delivery of spatial projects, ensuring high quality, compliance, and on-time outcomes.

Deliver high-quality cartographic outputs.

Manage spatial project proposals and, budgets.

Mentor junior team members and review their work to maintain technical excellence.

Design, execute, and document complex spatial analyses.

Independently manage large-scale projects with minimal supervision.

Review spatial data and mapping outputs produced by junior staff.

Maintain the integrity and quality of spatial data through regular audits.

 Qualifications and Experience

Proven experience using GIS platforms (e.g. ArcGIS) to perform raster and vector-based spatial analysis.

Demonstrated ability to deliver high-quality GIS outputs, including maps, visualisations, and spatial analysis to support project planning and client decision-making.

Experience compiling, maintaining, and improving spatial datasets and geospatial databases.

Exposure to geoprocessing workflows and scripting (e.g. Python) to support spatial analysis and mapping.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, with the ability to translate spatial data into clear, meaningful insights.

Experience preparing technical documentation, client reports, and supporting materials related to GIS tools and workflows.

Proven ability to work both independently and collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams to deliver projects on time.

Why Join Niche

Our people tell us they love being part of a great team where they feel a strong sense of belonging. That’s what drives us to support each other to do our best work.

Flexible work arrangements to support a work-life balance, including additional paid Life Leave to support your health, wellbeing, development and personal fulfilment.

An inclusive community of experts, advisors and mentors at your fingertips to support your growth and learning.

Collaborative, supportive work environment to help you to do the best work of your career and continue to grow.

Groups and social events to facilitate making meaningful connections.

Interesting and impactful work that leaves a lasting legacy.

A competitive salary based on skills and experience.

How to Apply

If this sounds like a great fit, please submit your application including a resume and cover letter addressing the key requirements to car••••@niche-eh.com

We do not accept Agency representations.

Niche is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We encourage qualified candidates of all differences to apply. We also recognise the same recruitment process doesn’t fit all, so if you require any accommodations or adjustments, simply let us know at the time you apply.

Applicants are required to have the right to work in Australia to be considered for this opportunity.

Job Title: Change Communications Analyst - Workplace / Workforce Relocation NSW Government
Ad Text:
$800 to $1,008 Per Day
Parramatta Location
3 days per week in office – Rest WFH
Initial 6 Months Contract
Immediate start
We are seeking an experienced Change Analyst with strong change communications, stakeholder engagement, SharePoint, feedback management and change impact assessment experience to support a large-scale workplace relocation and office transition program.

This role will suit a collaborative, agile and highly organised Change Analyst who is comfortable working across both change management and communications, with a strong ability to create practical materials, manage stakeholder feedback, analyse sentiment and support adoption during periods of operational change.

The role is approximately 50% change management and 50% communications, with a strong focus on workplace transition, physical / technical change, stakeholder readiness and employee engagement.

The project involves workplace planning, relocation activities, block-and-stack planning, floor moves, stakeholder engagement, vendor coordination, communications, feedback management and post-implementation hypercare.
  
About you

Proven experience as a Change Analyst or similar role on large organisational change, workplace change, transformation, relocation or transition programs.
Hands-on experience with SharePoint content management and feedback channel management.
Strong understanding of change management principles, frameworks and methodologies.
Experience implementing change and communications plans.
Strong ability to conduct or support change impact assessments.
Excellent written communication skills, including the ability to write, edit and simplify complex information for broad audiences.
Strong experience developing communication collateral such as FAQs, presentations, intranet content, emails, newsletters, articles and internal social posts.
Experience using Microsoft tools such as Teams, SharePoint and Viva Engage.
Strong stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills.
Ability to analyse stakeholder feedback, questions and sentiment to guide communication and change tactics.
Strong risk and issue management capability.
High attention to detail and strong documentation discipline.
Ability to work autonomously while also being collaborative, respectful, adaptable and team-focused.
Experience in any of the following would be highly regarded:

NSW Government or broader public sector experience.
Workplace relocation, office relocation, workplace transition or accommodation change projects.
Physical / technical change programs involving people, workplace, systems, process or operational impacts.
Large-scale stakeholder engagement across multiple business units.
Post-implementation support or hypercare.
Agile project environments.
Change management methodologies such as Prosci, ADKAR, Agile Change, Kotter or similar.
Key responsibilities

Partner with the Change Manager, project team and business stakeholders to implement change and communications plans.
Support change impact assessments to understand how workplace transition activities affect different stakeholder groups.
Create clear, practical and fit-for-purpose communication materials, including FAQs, emails, presentations, SharePoint content, intranet updates, learning materials, graphics, articles, newsletters and Viva Engage / internal social media posts.
Build, update and manage SharePoint sites and other internal communication channels.
Manage stakeholder feedback channels in line with agreed timeframes and service levels.
Analyse stakeholder questions, feedback, sentiment and themes to inform change and communication tactics.
Support stakeholder engagement activities, including workshops, briefings, lunch-and-learn sessions and on-the-ground support.
Identify and manage change risks, issues, resistance points and adoption barriers.
Work closely with project teams, vendors, business units and leadership stakeholders to support smooth implementation.
Attend site during key change periods and support post-implementation hypercare to help resolve queries and unforeseen issues.
Support documentation, planning, scheduling and coordination of change collateral and communication timelines.

At Macquarie University, we are hiring an Agile Coach who carries the gravitas to influence at an executive level and lead the establishment of a sustainable, fit-for-purpose agile operating model across our Deputy Vice-Chancellor (People and Operations) portfolio.

A style that embeds lasting capability, accelerates responsiveness and pace, and strengthens the connection across the portfolio. As an Agile Coach, you will partner with senior leaders to architect the squad structures, delivery rhythms, governance models, roles, and decision rights required to enable cross-functional delivery.

You will craft the strategy, steer its implementation, and drive disciplined execution, ultimately embedding the strategy into an operating model that is designed to sustain and thrive our agile ways of working.

The DVCPO portfolio is advancing multiple transformation initiatives requiring consistent adoption of agile and modern delivery practices across business and technology. The Student Lifecycle Program is a key priority, with a dedicated workstream to implement agile ways of working across Future Students, Admissions, and Group Marketing. In addition, this role will support other priority areas within the portfolio, including IT, contributing to the implementation of a DevOps operating model, Student Connect, and other areas embedding improved ways of working and delivery outcomes.

About the role

Reporting to our Executive Director (Strategy & Operations), you will move across priority functions within the DVCPO portfolio, with every engagement anchored to clear objectives, defined success measures, and purposeful knowledge transfer. Your key responsibilities will include:

Design and lead fit-for-purpose agile operating cadences across Future Students, Admissions, and Group Marketing, including planning cycles, prioritisation frameworks, showcases, and retrospectives.

Improve end-to-end flow across interdependent teams by clarifying roles, dependencies, handovers, and decision pathways.

Coach leaders and delivery teams to adopt agile practices that increase responsiveness, pace, and connection across the student lifecycle.

Coach teams on modern delivery and DevOps-aligned practices, including cross-functional ways of working, continuous improvement routines, and lean flow.

Establish common language, working agreements, and practical governance to support the DevOps operating model within IT and partner teams.

Partner with Student Connect leadership and teams to embed practical ways of working that address identified recommendations, enabling clarity of priorities and outcomes, and strengthening cross-team collaboration and delivery discipline.

Provide coaching and mentoring to product owners, delivery leads, team leads, and subject matter experts across the portfolio.

Develop playbooks, templates, and measures that streamline and standardise practice, creating consistent and efficient ways of working that reduce complexity, improve alignment, and support scalable delivery.

Establish and support communities of practice and knowledge transfer to ensure capability uplift is maintained.

About us

The Deputy Vice-Chancellor, People and Operations is responsible for driving strategic resource planning and workforce development, and for leading the delivery of the core professional functions of the University including student recruitment, marketing and communications, digital transformation, human resources, and student and corporate shared services.

About you (Selection Criteria)

You are an Agile practitioner who brings executive gravitas, navigates complexity with confidence, and embeds enduring, fit-for-purpose ways of working.

Extensive experience as an Agile Coach, Delivery Lead, or equivalent practitioner, with a demonstrated track record of embedding agile and modern delivery practices across multiple teams and functions simultaneously.

Deep practical knowledge of agile frameworks, lean principles, and DevOps-aligned ways of working, with the judgement to adapt approaches to organisational context rather than applying a one-size-fits-all model.

Proven ability to coach and influence senior leaders, product owners, and delivery leads.

Experience improving cross-team coordination, managing dependencies, and designing operating rhythms (planning cadences, prioritisation, retrospectives) that increase delivery predictability and flow.

Strong facilitation, communication, and relationship-building skills, with the ability to engage diverse stakeholders across business and technology functions.

Experience with developing practical artefacts such as playbooks, templates, communities of practice, that embed knowledge and reduce reliance on specialist support.

Familiarity with complex organisational change in a large institution, government, or university setting is highly regarded.

How to Apply

To be considered, please apply online with your CV and a cover letter outlining how your experience aligns with the selection criteria (above).

Applications Close

Applications will close on 2nd June at 11.00 PM (AEST), kindly please note we reserve the right to progress or decline an application prior to the application closing date.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Director, Product Simplification & Development for an initial 6-month contract based in Sydney, Australia. In this high-impact transformation role, you will spearhead the evolution of our product, pricing, and portfolio strategy to realize long-term strategic ambitions. You will translate enterprise direction into actionable roadmaps and design the next generation of customer-centric telecommunications experiences. Focused on the 1-3 year horizon, you will be responsible for defining the future product construct, driving portfolio rationalization, and unlocking new revenue streams through differentiated propositions.


Key Responsibilities
Product & Portfolio Strategy: Lead the ongoing refresh of the product strategy. Shape the strategic portfolio across products, experiences, channels, and capabilities to realize long-term growth ambitions.

Capability Building: Identify strategic capability gaps and evaluate build/partner/buy options to enable flawless execution.

Additionally, you will bring:
Strategic & Commercial Acumen: Proven mastery of structured problem-solving, financial valuation, and proposition/pricing economics.
Transformation Expertise: Experience designing and business-casing large-scale strategic initiatives, migrations, or portfolio-simplification programs.
Data-Driven Storytelling: The ability to synthesize market and customer insights into compelling narratives and robust business cases.
Influencing & Communication Skills: Exceptional executive presence with the ability to facilitate complex trade-offs and influence a diverse matrix of internal and external stakeholders.
People Leadership: A track record of leading, inspiring, and developing a high-performing team of strategic professionals.

What's in it for You?

This contract offers a unique seat at the table to genuinely reshape how a major telecommunications portfolio operates. You will hold direct accountability for driving portfolio value (ROI/NPV), generating revenue/margin uplift, and speeding up executive decision-making via top-tier strategic leadership.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



We help Australia's most iconic financial services organsiations maintain trust and navigate significant change, both internal and external. Organisations must protect customer and shareholder value and uphold commitments made to employees, regulators and the community.

The Risk and Regulatory team advises some of Australia's largest and most influential organisations on their governance, risk, compliance and control frameworks as well as the risk and regulatory impacts of major change.

Our goal is to deliver differentiated, transformational advice by harnessing the power of digital solutions and data. We believe in truly understanding the risks, issues and opportunities for our clients, and we offer industry insights to help build resilience and readiness in an uncertain environment.

What will your typical day look like?
Client delivery
• Advise clients on their operating model, embedding leading practice around governance, risks, controls and compliance.
• Problem-solve and project manage junior team members to help clients respond to emergent risks and regulation.
• Help clients deliver major enterprise change as they respond to risk and regulatory priorities, whilst protecting the interests of internal and external stakeholders.
• Manage client relationships during engagements.
• Present insights and recommendations to Senior Management and Executive stakeholders.
Team leader
• Play a passionate and meaningful role in developing the careers of team members by enhancing their governance, risks, controls and compliance capabilities.
• Manage high performing project teams and manage individual performance at a project level.
• Contribute to the culture and development of wider team of peers outside of a project environment and mentor junior staff as they develop their careers.
Business growth
• Manage projects with responsibility for quality, client service and project financials.
• Invest time building relationships and profile in the market and in the Firm.
• Contribute to the development of the business by taking an authentic interest in the challenging problems faced by existing and new clients.

About the team
We are a leader in the Sydney market, running and supporting large transformation projects with many iconic national brands. We operate an outward-looking and global practice, part of the Deloitte Asia Pacific firm, bringing insights from every continent to Sydney as well as providing our expertise across Australia and internationally.

Enough about us, let's talk about you.
You may have all or some of the following skills / experience:
Advisory skills

A working knowledge of Governance, Risks, Controls and Regulatory matters relevant to the Financial Services industry.
• Good commercial intelligence, an understanding of how large organisations operate.
• Passionate about making an impact.
• Experience in complex problem solving, consulting or business improvement preferred.
• Experience conducting analysis (desktop research, data analysis, reviewing documents etc.) and synthesising into digestible outputs (e.g. reports).
• Highly effective communication skills, both written and verbal, and the ability to build strong relationships.

Job Description

Advanced software engineering, coding, scripting, and SQL/database skills.
Skilled in logical and physical data modelling.
Hands‑on experience with DevOps practices — deployments, infrastructure, monitoring, and operations.
Demonstrated leadership in tech‑leading, mentoring, and developing engineers to enhance delivery performance.

The Senior Value Advisor is a high-impact, strategic partner to the sales organization and our most prestigious customers. In this role, you will act as a "Value Architect," moving beyond simple ROI spreadsheets to build compelling, multi-year business transformation narratives. You are responsible for driving the value-selling motion across the territory, serving as a thought leader who bridges the gap between Snowflake’s technical capabilities and a CXO’s strategic objectives.

You will not only lead complex value engagements but also scale our collective impact by mentoring the field, driving adoption of value selling and self-serve value tools, and championing the Snowflake value proposition at industry events. This is a role for a master storyteller who thrives at the intersection of business strategy, financial acumen, and persuasive communication.

What You Get To Do In This Role
Strategic Value Leadership & Deal Execution


Drive Account Strategy: Partner with Sales Leadership to architect account plans for high-value prospects, identifying "big bet" use cases that align Snowflake’s platform to the customer’s CEO-level priorities.

Lead Executive Discovery: Facilitate high-stakes value discovery workshops with CXOs and Business Unit leads to uncover pain points and map them to quantifiable business outcomes.

End-to-End Value Lifecycle: Own the delivery of comprehensive Value Assessments (Pre-sale) and Value Realization Studies (Post-sale) to ensure customers are not just buying software, but achieving measurable ROI.

Master the "3 Whys": Lead the field in answering Why Change? Why Snowflake? Why Now? to build urgency and defend against the "status quo" or competitors.

Sales Enablement & Scalability


Methodology Integration: Operationalize MEDDPICC within the value advisory and sales process to ensure deep qualification and alignment on Value Drivers.

Strengthen Value Motion: Act as a force multiplier by coaching Account Executives and SEs on value-selling methodologies, helping them move from "feature-function" selling to "outcome-based" selling.

Tooling & Innovation: Deploy and drive enablement of self-service value assets, ROI calculators, and FinOps frameworks that allow the broader field organization to lead with value independently.

Thought Leadership & Visual Storytelling


Public Advocacy: Represent Snowflake as a speaker at industry conferences, executive roundtables, and webinars, promoting our unique differentiation in the data cloud market.

Collateral Creation: Author whitepapers, case studies, and internal "playbooks" that distill complex economic concepts into simple, visually compelling narratives.

Required Qualifications
Experience & Expertise


Proven Track Record: 5–8+ years of experience in Value Engineering, Customer Success, Management Consulting (Tier 1 firms preferred), or a strategic "Value-Added" Sales role.

Executive Presence: Masterful presentation skills with the ability to command a room of C-suite executives and pivot conversations from technical hurdles to business value.

Financial & Analytical Mastery: Deep expertise in financial modeling (NPV, ROI, TCO, and IRR). You should be able to defend complex business cases under scrutiny from a customer’s CFO.

Skills & Knowledge


Business Transformation: Strong understanding of enterprise cloud economics and the business impact of modern data architectures (e.g., Data Sharing, AI/ML monetization, and Data Governance).

FinOps Understanding: Knowledge and understanding of Cloud FinOps and consumption-based economic models.

Visual Storytelling: Exceptional ability to translate data into "boardroom-ready" visual presentations that tell a clear, persuasive story.

Education, Travel & Mindset


Education: Undergraduate degree in Economics, Finance, Engineering, or Business.

Travel: Ability to travel 25%–35% to engage with customers on-site and lead industry-level engagements.

Mindset: A go-getter, naturally curious and collaborative demeanour

About Zinfra: 

Zinfra is a leading partner in the design, build, operation and maintenance of Australia’s energy and utilities infrastructure. Delivering over $1B portfolio annually, the work we do is essential to bringing energy to our communities safely and reliably, while powering the nation’s energy transition. With a diverse workforce of more than 2,300, we expertly deliver our services across Australia. 

About the role:  

We’re seeking a commercially minded analyst to join our fast-paced Commercial team, with a focus on improving contract performance across complex infrastructure and services environments.

This role is suited to individuals transitioning from infrastructure advisory, transactions, or asset management within Big 4, who are looking to move closer to operational outcomes and tangible commercial impact.

Working closely with senior commercial leaders, you’ll apply a diagnostic, insight-led approach to understanding contract performance—translating financial and operational data into clear commercial actions that protect margin, improve outcomes, and strengthen decision-making.

A key part of the role involves evolving commercial processes and tools, using structured analysis to support standardisation, automation, and digital enablement across the contract lifecycle.

What you’ll be doing

Analysing contract performance to identify drivers of margin erosion, underperformance, and commercial risk
Translating data into clear, actionable commercial insights, supporting senior stakeholders in decision making
Developing concise commercial insight packs (similar to client deliverables), linking financial outcomes to contractual mechanisms
Partnering with Commercial Managers to diagnose issues across live contracts and recommend practical, outcome-focused solutions
Supporting the modernisation of commercial operations, including workflow mapping, templatisation, and claims process optimisation
Contributing to the design and rollout of digital tools and automated workflows that improve commercial visibility and consistency
Working cross-functionally with Finance, Operations, Process Improvement, and Digital teams
Helping embed scalable analytical approaches and tools across the commercial function

About you

You bring a foundation in structured problem solving and commercial analysis, with experience in environments where you’ve assessed performance, identified issues, and supported senior stakeholders with insight-driven recommendations.

You’ll likely have:

Experience in Big 4 (KPMG, PwC, Deloitte, EY), ideally within Infrastructure Advisory, Transactions, or Asset Management
Exposure to commercial performance analysis, such as reviewing project performance, identifying value leakage, or supporting investment/commercial decisions
Experience producing structured analysis and client-ready materials (e.g. decks, models, insight packs)
1–2 years in industry (or strong exposure to real asset environments), giving you an appreciation of operational and contractual realities
Strong financial modelling and analytical skills, with the ability to link numbers to commercial outcomes
An interest in moving from advisory into a role with direct influence on contract performance and profitability
Exposure to process improvement, standardisation, or digital enablement initiatives is advantageous

Why this role

Transition from advisory into a role with direct, tangible commercial impact
Work on live contracts and real performance challenges, rather than retrospective analysis
Gain exposure to end-to-end commercial lifecycle management
Contribute to modernising commercial operations, including automation and digital tools
Partner closely with experienced commercial leaders and influence real business outcomes

Why you should work for us 

What we do matters. Delivering safe and reliable energy makes a difference to people’s lives across our country every single day. We are proud to keep Australia connected while preparing the nation for a renewable future. Our people are our energy source. We offer meaningful benefits and rewards, including being a certified family-friendly workplace, flexible work arrangements, various leave benefits, career development pathways, a wellbeing program, and more. 

We know Zinfra is a great place to belong—but don’t just take our word for it; hear our team members share what working at Zinfra means to them.  

We champion and value diversity in life and work experiences, as we know everyone benefits from broader diversity of thoughts in our problem solving, decision making and ability to innovate. We encourage applications from everyone, including First Nations people, veterans, people living with a disability and members of the LGBTIQA+ community. If you need adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know. 

Recruitment Process 

During our recruitment process, you may be required to undertake pre-employment checks including pre-employment medical, reference checks and a national police check. 

Notice to Third Parties: Zinfra does not accept unsolicited resumes (or liability associated with fees or costs) from recruitment agencies, search firms or third parties. Interested candidates are welcome to submit their application independently.

Job Title: Senior Leader - Program Design and Delivery
Ad Text:
Join Us to Deliver Work That Matters
Join our Program Design and Delivery team in Canberra as a Senior Leader and take a leading role in shaping and delivering major Federal Government programs. This is a senior client-facing role for someone who brings deep P3M experience, strong executive relationships and a genuine interest in building high-performing teams that deliver practical outcomes.


You will lead a large multidisciplinary team embedded with a Federal Government client, while also helping grow our broader Federal Government P3M capability. Alongside delivery, you will work closely with Partners and leaders across Synergy to identify opportunities, respond to client needs, support proposals and help build a strong pipeline of future work. 


What you’ll do: 
This is a role for a hands-on leader who can confidently balance strategy, delivery, people leadership and business growth. In practice, this looks like:
Lead the delivery of a major Federal Government program and a large multidisciplinary client team.
Manage program planning, schedules, resourcing, risks, issues and dependencies.
Build trusted relationships with senior client stakeholders and act as a strategic delivery partner.
Coach and lead P3M professionals, supporting team performance, growth and career development.
Help grow Synergy’s Federal Government P3M practice through workforce planning and talent development.
Identify opportunities, shape proposals and contribute to new work across major programs.
What you’ll bring: 
Our most successful people are curious, collaborative problem-solvers who bring structured thinking, a growth mindset and the resilience to thrive in fast-moving environments. They build trusted relationships with integrity, communicate clearly and know when to step in to help deliver great outcomes. In this role, you’ll also bring: 
Extensive experience (ideally 12+ years) leading high-performing program teams across multiple disciplines within professional services organisations operating in Federal Government environments.
Strong and active relationships with senior executives across Federal Government.
Contemporary program and project management certifications, with strong P3M discipline.
A track record of building, leading and retaining capable consulting teams.
Experience identifying opportunities and contributing to proposals in a professional services setting.
Clear communication, sound judgement and the ability to adapt your approach to different clients and contexts.

Due to the nature of the work, the successful candidate must hold, or be eligible to obtain and maintain, a Baseline security clearance. If you already hold a current Baseline or higher clearance, or are eligible and willing to undergo AGSVA security vetting, we encourage you to apply. Please note that Australian citizenship is required for this process.

At Synergy Group, we’re challengers, collaborators, and problem solvers. Our culture is built on curiosity, creativity, integrity, and a belief in potential. We partner closely with clients to shape solutions that tackle Australia’s most complex challenges, and we attract people who want their work to matter. You’ll join a team that values trust, growth, and meaningful impact.

Benefits to support different life stages, including generous parental leave and purchased leave options.
Everyday benefits through Flare discounts, novated leasing, and a range of salary sacrificing options.
A collaborative office environment that promotes innovation, plus the perks of a fully stocked kitchen (snacks and barista coffee), a dedicated kids’ space, and a few ways to unwind (including the ping pong table).
Learning and growth support, including access to LinkedIn Learning.

About Bower Insights

Bower Insights is a forward-thinking property advisory consultancy specialising in development management, property strategy, economics, policy and research.

We exist to bridge the gap between policy intent and delivery, helping organisations turn vision into impact. We believe in the power of informed decisions, and in the people behind them, to create lasting change. Our purpose is to provide insights and services that enable our clients to achieve positive outcomes.

Our integrated expertise allows us to support clients across the full property and development lifecycle — from strategy and advisory, through procurement and transaction management, to delivery oversight. We are a growing team based in Melbourne's CBD, working on high-impact projects across Victoria and nationally.

Find out more at www.bowerinsights.au




About the role

Bower Insights is seeking a senior development management professional who can provide expert, independent advice to our clients on property strategy, development procurement and transaction management. This is a consulting and advisory role rather than a traditional in-house project delivery position.

The Development Manager will work across a portfolio of client engagements, helping organisations navigate complex property decisions by preparing high-quality written advice, options analyses, procurement strategies and transaction support. Where clients require active delivery management, the role will also provide oversight and coordination of development projects, but the defining value of this position is the advisory expertise you bring and your ability to communicate it clearly and credibly.

You will work closely with government agencies, community housing providers, institutional investors and not-for-profit clients across residential, mixed-use, affordable housing and urban renewal projects.




Key responsibilities

Provide expert advisory services to clients on property strategy, opportunity assessment, project structuring and development pathways.

Prepare high-quality written outputs including strategy reports, options papers, procurement strategies, risk assessments and transaction briefs for senior client and stakeholder audiences.

Advise clients on development partner procurement, including design and management of EOI, RFP and BAFO processes.

Support clients through transaction management activities, including due diligence, deal structuring and negotiation support.

Assist clients in developing and testing development feasibilities, financial models and project business cases.

Provide independent advice on development agreements, commercial structures and consultant and construction contracts.

Advise on asset strategy and portfolio planning, including development opportunity identification and delivery pathway recommendations.

Where acting as delivery manager, oversee project programs, budgets, risks and governance reporting on behalf of clients.

Contribute to government funding submissions, housing program applications and policy-facing documents.

Support the growth of Bower Insights through client engagement, proposal preparation and business development.




About you

You are an experienced development management professional who has moved beyond pure delivery — you understand the strategic, commercial and advisory dimensions of property, and you can communicate complex development matters clearly in writing and in person.




You will bring:

Minimum 7 years' experience in property development, development advisory, client-side project management or a related consulting field.

Demonstrated experience providing advice to government, institutional or not-for-profit clients on property strategy, procurement or transactions.

Proven ability to prepare polished, professional written advisory outputs for senior client audiences.

Strong track record in development procurement processes, including EOI and RFP design and evaluation.

Experience with development feasibility modelling, commercial structuring and legal frameworks.

Knowledge of affordable housing, social housing or government-funded development programs highly regarded.

Tertiary qualification in Property Development, Urban Planning, Construction Management, Commerce or a related discipline.

Exceptional written communication skills and a strong commitment to quality advisory outputs.

The ability to manage multiple client engagements concurrently with autonomy and sound professional judgement.




Why Bower Insights

At Bower Insights, we believe in creating a workplace where people thrive. Our culture is built on collaboration, innovation, and respect. We value diversity and encourage fresh ideas that help us deliver meaningful insights to our clients.

We are a people-focused business, and supporting our staff to do work they enjoy and grow professionally is a core component of our approach.

What we offer:

A genuinely advisory consulting role — your expertise and written counsel are the product.

High-profile, complex and meaningful projects across housing, urban development and government advisory.

A collaborative, intellectually engaged team environment in Melbourne's CBD.

Opportunities for senior leadership and professional growth as the firm expands.

Flexible working arrangements and a supportive culture.

Competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience.

The opportunity to contribute to work that creates lasting social and community impact.

The Change Manager ensures the people side of a client’s software development lifecycle (SDLC) uplift is delivered with rigour, driving adoption, readiness, and measurable value realisation. This role enables delivery teams to consistently apply evolving standards, playbooks, and patterns by building the adoption system, running the Change & Adoption Network, and co-delivering training and enablement.

WHAT GOOD LOOKS LIKE 

Delivery teams use the agreed SDLC patterns consistently and know where to find them.
The Change & Adoption Network is active and increasingly self-sustaining with clear cadence.
Training and onboarding land effectively for teams and new joiners.
Adoption measures and feedback show lift (reduced friction, faster delivery, more consistent outcomes).

WHAT YOU’LL DO 

Own day-to-day change and adoption delivery for SDLC uplift, ensuring adoption activities are planned, executed, and tracked across delivery teams.
Partner with client leads and project domain leads to package and roll out evolving best practices across project teams, coordinating waves/releases and adoption milestones.
Operate and continuously improve the adoption “system”: cadence, governance touchpoints, dependency tracking, and a prioritised adoption backlog (comms, enablement, champions actions, leadership alignment).
Run the Change & Adoption Network (champions model), including cadence and sessions.
Design and coordinate training and enablement (role-based pathways and supporting assets) so teams can adopt the practices consistently, including onboarding for new teams/new joiners.
Gather feedback and usage signals from delivery teams and the network, remove adoption blockers, and work with client leads and project domain leads to iterate practices to be fit-for-purpose.
Run targeted impact checks when practices/patterns are introduced or updated (who is affected, what changes, what support is required) to keep rollout pragmatic and focused.
Track and report progress through program governance (adoption measures, risks/issues, dependencies) and highlight where additional intervention is needed to drive uptake and sustainment.

EXPERIENCE 

Extensive experience leading end-to-end change management for complex transformations (business and technology), with strong ownership from discovery through adoption and sustainment. 
Deep, hands-on capability across core change disciplines: change planning, impact and readiness, stakeholder and sponsor engagement, communications, learning/enablement, and adoption measurement. 
Proven ability to translate strategy into practical, fit-for-context change plans and deliver them in fast-moving delivery environments. 
Proven ability to work effectively with technical teams and leaders to embed new ways of working into day-to-day delivery  
Excellent facilitation and influencing skills across senior leaders, SMEs, and delivery teams. 
Strong governance and reporting discipline, including dependency management, risk/issue management, and clear progress reporting against adoption/readiness measures.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS 

Certifications: Prosci / ACMP / APMG (or equivalent).
Experience working with technology delivery teams.
Exposure to AI-enabled ways of working and adoption patterns.

Senior Technical Business Analyst

The Senior Technical Business Analyst bridges the gap between business stakeholders and engineering teams. They are responsible for identifying, documenting, and communicating project requirements across business, technology, and user dimensions. They have a strong emphasis on technical depth across digital delivery contexts including APIs, mobile applications, front-end platforms, and software engineering practices.

The Senior Technical BA works fluidly within agile delivery teams, understanding how software is designed, built, tested, and deployed. They bring enough technical fluency to engage credibly with engineers, QA teams, and architects, and enough business acumen to translate complex technical constraints into clear, actionable outcomes for customers and stakeholders.

Areas of Primary Responsibility

Serve as the key interface between customers/stakeholders and engineering teams, translating business needs into clear technical requirements.
Build and maintain trusted customer relationships through discovery, delivery, and ongoing improvement.
Lead and facilitate workshops, discovery sessions, and requirement reviews with technical and non-technical audiences.
Elicit, analyse, and document user, business, technology, and stakeholder requirements; confirm scope and expected outcomes.
Create and maintain delivery artefacts including epics, user stories, acceptance criteria, process maps, data flows, and integration/API documentation.
Ensure backlog items are well-formed, prioritised, estimated, and "ready" for engineering with minimal ambiguity.
Set up and maintain documentation aligned to Versent/client standards, templates, and best practices.
Partner with engineers and architects to understand solution options, evaluate trade-offs, and validate feasibility.
Communicate and clarify API contracts (REST/GraphQL), request/response patterns, authentication, and integration dependencies.
Collaborate with front-end and mobile teams, considering UI behaviour, state, platform constraints (iOS/Android), and UX impacts on requirements.
Capture functional and non-functional requirements (performance, security, scalability, reliability) and ensure they are traceable through delivery.
Actively participate in agile ceremonies (planning, stand-ups, refinement, showcases, retrospectives) and contribute to delivery cadence.
Manage the product backlog in Jira (story/epic structure, linking/labelling, boards, and reporting) to support delivery transparency.
Work with QA to ensure requirements are testable; write acceptance criteria suitable for BDD/Gherkin and support defect triage.
Identify, track, and escalate risks, issues, dependencies, and scope gaps early; align requirements to customer strategy and business goals.

Skills & Experience

Required

5+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, with at least 2 years in a technically complex digital delivery environment.
Jira proficiency: hands-on experience managing backlogs, epics, sprints, custom fields, dashboards, and reporting in Jira Software.
API literacy: ability to read and interpret REST API documentation, Swagger/OpenAPI specs, and understand request/response patterns, HTTP methods, status codes, and authentication (OAuth, API keys).
Mobile application familiarity: experience delivering requirements for native iOS/Android or cross-platform mobile apps (React Native, Flutter, or similar), with awareness of platform-specific constraints.
Front-end framework awareness: familiarity with modern front-end frameworks (React, Angular, Vue, or similar) and the ability to engage with front-end engineers on component behaviour, state, and UI logic.
Dev team fluency: demonstrated experience working embedded within agile engineering squads, contributing to ceremonies, and understanding the software development lifecycle end-to-end.
QA automation understanding: ability to write testable acceptance criteria (ideally in BDD/Gherkin) and collaborate with QA automation engineers on test coverage and defect management.
Strong skills in user story writing, process mapping, gap analysis, and requirements documentation.
Experience with stakeholder management across technical and non-technical audiences.
Confident communicator: able to explain technical concepts to business stakeholders and business context to engineers.

Desirable

Exposure to cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and understanding of cloud-native application patterns.
Experience with Confluence, Miro, Figma (review/comment), or similar collaboration tools.
Familiarity with CI/CD tools such as GitHub Actions, Bitbucket Pipelines, or AWS CodePipeline.
Experience in professional services or consulting environments.
Exposure to security and identity requirements (IAM, SSO, MFA, data privacy).
Knowledge of integration patterns (event-driven, pub/sub, message queues).
Agile certifications (CSM, CSPO, SAFe) or BA certifications (IIBA CBAP/CCBA).

About UNSW:

UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. We’re a large organisation with a diverse and talented community; a community doing extraordinary things. The Research Data Systems Capability Lead is responsible for shaping, delivering and continuously improving UNSW’s enterprise research data platforms and services.

This role sits at the intersection of research and technology, ensuring data capabilities align to the research lifecycle while being practical, scalable and fit-for-purpose. It requires strong stakeholder engagement to bring together researchers, IT and governance functions, translating complex needs into implementable outcomes.

A key focus of the role is supporting the in-flight Research Data Experience (RDE) program, including translating researcher needs into solutions, running pilots and proofs-of-concept, and progressing capabilities into scalable, production-ready services.

The role balances innovation and experimentation with secure, sustainable and well-governed technology practices, aligning with UNSW’s strategic focus on enabling research excellence through responsible digital capabilities.

What you’ll focus on

Lead the design, delivery and continuous improvement of research data platforms and services
Work across researchers, IT and governance to translate needs into deliverable outcomes
Drive pilots, proofs-of-concept and iterative delivery as part of the RDE program
Shape priorities and outcomes, progressing capabilities from early experimentation to scalable services
Facilitate alignment across stakeholders to navigate complexity and enable decision-making
Ensure solutions are usable, compliant, and sustainable, leveraging automation and emerging capabilities (including AI) to improve researcher experience and reduce administrative burden

For a full outline of responsibilities and expectations, please refer to the Position Description.

About You

Success in this role comes from your ability to work across research and technology, building trusted relationships and bringing diverse stakeholders together to deliver practical outcomes.

You have strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills, a solid understanding of the research lifecycle, and the ability to translate needs into clear, implementable solutions.

You take a delivery-focused approach and apply sound judgement to balance innovation with secure, sustainable and scalable solutions, seeing technology as an enabler of research outcomes, and are comfortable navigating both research and technology environments, aligning different ways of working to deliver outcomes that are safe, compliant and aligned with UNSW’s strategic direction.

Skills and Experience (Essential)

Exceptional stakeholder engagement and influencing capability, with the ability to navigate complex, multi-stakeholder environments and align competing priorities, perspectives and objectives to achieve outcomes
Proven ability to bridge research and technology domains, translating researcher needs into implementable solutions and articulating technical concepts back to non-technical stakeholders
Experience shaping and managing platforms or services, including setting priorities, defining outcomes, and driving delivery through iterative improvement
Strong understanding of the research lifecycle and how data platforms support data capture, management, analysis, sharing and reuse
Strong judgement and ability to operate effectively in complex environments, managing ambiguity, competing priorities and driving outcomes

Flexible Working Options (work from home, flexible hours etc) 
Career development opportunities
17% Superannuation contributions and additional leave loading payments 
Additional 3 days of leave over Christmas period
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness)

About the Role

We are seeking a highly experienced Lead Underwriter - Travel (AU & NZ) to drive technical excellence, underwriting discipline, and portfolio performance across our Travel Line of Business.

In this senior role, you will oversee case underwriting for both new and renewing large commercial and global client deals, ensuring market competitiveness, profitability, and regulatory alignment. You will play a critical role in portfolio remediation, pricing governance, and strategic underwriting decisions, while partnering closely with Sales, Pricing, Actuarial, Product, and Portfolio teams across the region.

This role is ideal for an underwriting leader who thrives in complex environments, enjoys working across borders, and is ready to contribute to a business embracing digital and AI-enabled underwriting transformation.

Key Responsibilities

Case Underwriting & Portfolio Leadership

Oversee Case Underwriting activities across Australia, New Zealand, and Global Clients
Partner closely with Sales teams on large and complex new business and renewal opportunities
Lead underwriting for large commercial and banking client portfolios within AU & NZ
Ensure accurate pricing of deals and facilitate APAC deals requiring L6 or higher approvals
Support capacity planning and prioritisation aligned with Travel LoB strategy

Pricing, Performance & Remediation

Collaborate with Pricing to monitor tariff-based portfolio performance and initiate corrective actions where required
Lead remediation activities for underperforming accounts in line with portfolio monitoring insights
Provide timely and accurate performance data for central portfolio monitoring
Support commercial margin projections and planning activities at portfolio and client level

Governance, Compliance & Risk

Ensure adherence to Allianz Standard for Underwriting and AzP underwriting guidelines
Oversee fulfilment of action items arising from conditional Product Risk Assessment approvals
Maintain strong alignment with Actuarial teams to support reserve adequacy and portfolio visibility
Ensure regulatory and underwriting governance standards are embedded and sustained

Product, Digital & AI Enablement

Partner with Product teams on Simplicity product rollout and future Travel product enhancements
Champion the adoption and effective use of digital underwriting tools (e.g. Salesforce, Pricing Tools)
Demonstrate AI readiness, including:
Comfort working with data-driven insights and predictive outputs
Supporting AI-enabled pricing, portfolio monitoring, and underwriting decision support tools
Contributing to the continuous improvement of underwriting processes through automation and analytics
Act as a role model for digital-first, insight-led underwriting practices

Collaboration & Leadership

Work effectively with virtual, multinational teams across APAC and MEA
Build strong partnerships across Sales, Pricing, Actuarial, Product, and Portfolio functions
Provide technical guidance and thought leadership within the underwriting community
About You

Essential Experience & Qualifications

Actuarial, financial, or equivalent analytical background
Minimum 5 years' experience in underwriting, portfolio monitoring, pricing, or related insurance disciplines
Strong experience dealing with complex commercial accounts
Proven ability to operate in multinational, virtual team environments
Fluent in English with strong stakeholder communication skills

Desirable

Experience within Travel Insurance
Exposure to banking or financial institution clients
Experience supporting or leading underwriting transformation initiatives

Capabilities for the Future

High level of digital literacy and AI readiness

Methodology Expertise: Strong understanding and practical experience with Agile and Waterfall 

Tools Proficiency: Skilled in using JIRA, Confluence, MIRO, Microsoft Teams, and other collaborative tools.

Ability to manage multiple priorities and work to deadlines
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Experience within transport, logistics, or related industries is highly regarded

Ad Text:
Step into a role where insight meets impact. About QBCC 

As Queensland's independent building and construction regulator, we play a critical role in protecting people, supporting industry and building trust in a system that touches every part of our community, from safe homes and liveable communities to jobs and economic security.

We support a $69 billion industry by protecting consumers, ensuring the industry is well supported, providing a safety net through the Home Warranty Scheme, and proactively delivering education.  

A thriving building and construction industry benefits us all. But it only works when people feel informed, enabled and protected. That's where we come in.

We support industry, protect consumers and uphold standards. We make fair and transparent decisions, deliver services that build trust, and support a strong, sustainable industry.

About the role  

This role sits at the intersection of insight, strategy and delivery, offering the autonomy to lead meaningful change. As part of the newly created Transformation and Change Division, you'll work closely with the Project Delivery, Transformation Management Office and Business Analytics and Intelligence branches to drive end-to-end improvement projects. You'll apply strong governance while leveraging data and research to shape smarter regulatory outcomes.

Partnering across QBCC, you'll design, deliver and evaluate strategic initiatives that challenge current practices and elevate performance. Suited to a self-directed professional, this role empowers you to take ownership, think critically and turn insight into action.

While the role allows for flexibility, weekly in-person attendance at the Brisbane office is an important part of this role.

You're a commercially astute professional who operates with confidence and autonomy, bringing a sharp, analytical lens to complex challenges. With a strong track record in project delivery, business improvement or change - ideally within a regulatory environment, you bring a pragmatic, outcomes-driven approach. ASC is Australia’s submarine company. We built the Royal Australian Navy’s Collins Class submarines, sustain them, and are now delivering their Life of Type Extension to increase the boats’ service lives. We have also been selected by the Australian Government to sustain and jointly build the nation’s conventionally armed, nuclear-powered submarines – the largest and most complex industrial undertaking in Australia’s history. 

Our work is interesting and meaningful, contributing directly to Australia’s national security. We offer rewarding roles that provide the opportunity to grow your career, and the flexibility to balance life and work. A career at ASC is like no other, delivering a deeper purpose we can all be proud of. 

About the Opportunity

We are seeking talented Knowledge Management professionals to join our small, growing team in support of ASC’s pathway to nuclear readiness. As part of a newly established function, this is a unique opportunity to play a hands-on role in shaping how knowledge is captured, shared, and leveraged across a complex and mission-critical environment. This role will contribute to the design, build, and growth of KM capability from the ground up. 

These are full-time opportunities, that can be based at our Osborne site in SA or Henderson in WA 

We are recruiting for two positions: 

1 × Senior Knowledge Management Specialist 
1 × Knowledge Management Specialist 

Both roles will contribute to building a strong knowledge management culture, delivering impactful initiatives, and ensuring critical knowledge is preserved and accessible across the organisation. 

About the roles 

Senior Knowledge Management Specialist 

Reporting to the Knowledge Manager, the Senior Knowledge Management Specialist leads complex, enterprise-wide knowledge management initiatives and helps evolve ASC’s KM capability. 

Key focus areas include: 

Leading cross-functional KM initiatives across the full knowledge lifecycle 
Supporting development and execution of KM strategy aligned to business priorities 
Uplifting KM capability across the organisation 
Driving KM governance, frameworks, and compliance aligned to ISO 30401 and defence requirements 
Influencing senior stakeholders and leading cultural change in knowledge sharing 
This role may include leadership responsibilities and direct reports in the future but currently they don’t 

Knowledge Management Specialist 

Reporting to the Knowledge Manager, the Knowledge Management Specialist supports the delivery of KM initiatives and embedding of KM practices across the business. 

Key focus areas include: 

Supporting and delivering cross-functional KM projects and initiatives 
Capturing and codifying critical knowledge from subject matter experts  
Managing knowledge assets, taxonomy, metadata, and content lifecycle  
Facilitating communities of practice, lessons learned, and knowledge sharing activities  
Providing training, support and guidance to uplift KM capability  

About You 

For both roles 

Tertiary qualifications in Knowledge Management, Information Systems, Engineering, Project/Change Management, or a similar discipline or equivalent experience 
Strong experience in knowledge capture, transfer, and content management practices  
Experience working in complex project, engineering or heavy industry environments (ideally from a defence environment) 
Ability to translate complex or technical information into accessible knowledge assets  
Experience with digital collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Confluence, PLM platforms) 
You are a positive, motivated individual 

Additional for Senior KM role 

7+ years’ experience in knowledge management 
Proven ability to lead enterprise-level KM initiatives and influence senior stakeholders  
Experience developing KM frameworks, governance, and maturity uplift approaches  
Demonstrated leadership, coaching, and mentoring capability 

Additional for KM role 

5+ years’ experience in a relevant technical or project environment  
Strong capability in facilitating KM processes such as lessons learned and communities of practice  

Eligibility 

This role requires a current National Police Clearance and an Australian Defence Security Clearance to a NV1 level. Your eligibility for this role is dependent on you obtaining and maintaining such clearances. 

To check your eligibility please visit the Australian Government Security Vetting Agency website. 

Benefits 

Unique and challenging work with a deeper purpose. 
Flexibility over how, when and where you work for the best work/life balance. 
Up to 10 days additional leave every year so you can rest and recharge. 
Generous parental leave so you can give your family the focus it deserves. 
Subsidised childcare to ease the financial pressure on your family. 

Apply today for a career with a deeper purpose.

Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

The IRRBB & Liquid Assets Analyst supports the Head of Balance Sheet & Capital in managing the Bank’s interest rate risk in the banking book (IRRBB), structural hedging activities and long‑term liquid asset portfolio. The role provides analysis, monitoring, recommendations and execution across IRRBB exposures, hedge performance, liquid asset investments and overall balance sheet positioning. It contributes to the development and maintenance of the Bank’s IRRBB framework and liquid asset strategy, including analysis of repricing, basis and yield curve risks, hedge effectiveness and investment performance to support informed risk management and decision‑making.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s most trusted bank and a leading purpose-driven business.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Analyse and monitor IRRBB exposures (repricing, basis and yield curve risk), supporting framework maintenance, stress testing and risk reporting (e.g. NIIS, EVE).
Analyse the Bank’s IRRBB profile and prepare derivative hedging actions.  Subject to individual dealing mandate negotiate and executed derivative hedging transactions with wholesale counterparties.
Support development and implementation of structural hedging strategies, including analysis of swaps, derivatives and hedge effectiveness.
Manage and provide insights on the long‑term liquid asset portfolio, assessing composition, duration, yield, liquidity and investment opportunities.
Monitor and manage counterparty credit risk exposures, including limits, utilisation, ratings and emerging risks.
Support balance sheet forecasting and scenario analysis, providing insights on pricing, funding, growth and market impacts on IRRBB and liquidity outcomes.
Prepare high‑quality reporting, analysis and recommendations for senior stakeholders, including ALCO and the Group Treasurer.
Maintain governance frameworks, policies and limits relating to IRRBB, hedging and liquid assets, ensuring alignment with regulatory expectations.
Collaborate across Treasury, Finance, Risk and external stakeholders to support effective risk management, decision‑making and regulatory engagement.

What you will bring

Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex interest rate risk, liquid asset portfolios and market dynamics.
High attention to detail and ownership of outputs, with the ability to identify, assess and escalate emerging risks.
Ability to translate technical Treasury concepts into clear, practical recommendations for stakeholders.
Collaborative approach, working effectively across Treasury, Finance, Risk and Technology teams.
Solid understanding of IRRBB, structural hedging, and long‑term liquid asset portfolio management (e.g. bonds, FRNs, duration, yield curve risk).
Strong financial modelling capability, including advanced Excel and experience with Treasury/ALM systems (e.g. QRM or equivalent).
Knowledge of prudential standards (e.g. APS 117, APS 210) and experience operating within regulated ADI environments.
Tertiary qualification in finance, economics, commerce or related discipline, with experience in Treasury, financial markets or balance sheet management highly regarded.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

Ad Text:
Our Client

Yumbah is proudly positioned as Australia’s leading aquaculture company. With operations across South Australia, Tasmania, Victoria and New South Wales, they sustainably farm abalone, oysters, mussels and kingfish for both domestic and international markets, and supply oyster spat to the South Australian oyster industry. Driven by their core values of integrity, daring and teamwork, we are committed to supporting our people and delivering a sustainable source of premium seafood protein to our customers.

An opportunity now exists for an experienced Senior Financial Analyst – FP&A to join the team based in Kent Town on a full time, permanent basis.

The Role

Yumbah is a growth-stage aquaculture business in a period of rapid expansion, supported by both acquisitions and ongoing organic growth. This trajectory is underpinned by a finance function that is continuing to be enhanced.

This role acts as the dedicated FP&A lead for a species division, with exposure across other areas over time. Partnering closely with the FP&A Manager, Species GM, and COO, you will own divisional planning, fair value assessment, integrated budgeting and forecasting, and monthly performance reporting to the executive and Board. You’ll be responsible for:

Translate operational and biological performance into clear financial outcomes for the assigned species division
Own the monthly Mass Balance refresh, S&OP cycle, and fair value assessment
Build and maintain the integrated three-way financial model for the division
Partner with the Species GM, COO, and operational leaders on budget, forecast, capex, and pricing decisions
Provide constructive challenge on variance drivers, forecasts, and operational assumptions
Contribute to the ongoing uplift of the FP&A function alongside the CFO and FP&A Manager
Act as the analytical backbone for a highly complex, operationally driven aquaculture business during a significant growth phase
Play a key role in shaping and strengthening a finance function in active build
About You

5 + years of experience as a Senior Analyst with strong financial modelling, business partnering and commercial advisory experience
Sector experience is not essential to be considered. Yumbah will invest in teaching you this providing your modelling and analytical foundations are strong.
Candidates from FMCG manufacturing, agribusiness, mining and resources, energy, infrastructure, healthcare, financial services, professional services and big 4 are all encouraged to apply
Architect's instinct. You think in systems and dependencies before detail. You design for replication rather than solving in isolation.
Constructive challenger. You are willing to push back on operational assumptions, capex business cases and forecast inputs with evidence-based analysis, while keeping working relationships intact.
Operational fluency. You will be on pens, in hatcheries, walking oyster racks. Good FP&A here is impossible without operational context, and that means time on site, not just time in spreadsheets.
Tech curiosity. You treat AI-assisted modelling, BI tools and workflow automation as professional leverage, not buzzwords or threats. You will help evolve our toolkit, not just use it.
Builder mindset. You are energised by ambiguity and active build, not destabilised by it. Templates and methodology will change during your tenure. You will help shape that change.
In Return

Strong opportunity to develop your expertise in integrated, three-way modelling across a complex, national business and AASB141 fair value methodology
AI and tech forward business with a focus on continuous improvement
Direct exposure to acquisition integration during the largest growth period in the company’s history
CFO adjacent visibility on a key species, with line of sight into Board reporting, audit interaction and strategic decision making
Adelaide based role in Kent Town with travel to regional and operational sites as required
Remuneration is competitive and aligned to a senior FP&A role of this complexity
Next Steps
 
If you’re interested in the role on offer, please apply using the relevant link. If you have any questions relating to the role, please email Megan Kivell – mki••••@ppsconsulting.com.au

AEMO at the Heart of Energy 

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role  

The WA CIA team is responsible to operate the Reserve Capacity Mechanism (RCM), a unique feature of the Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM) and pivotal to its operations. The RCM ensures sufficient energy producing systems and demand side management capacity is available to support reliable electricity supply in the WA grid.  

In this dynamic role, you will get to work with a smart, diverse and supportive group of people while actively leading and contributing to key processes that have a significant financial outcome on the WEM and its stakeholders. You will be actively supporting important reform initiatives aimed at transitioning WA’s electricity sector to a zero-carbon future.  

Be prepared for a cerebral workout most days as you use your expertise and regulatory understanding of the WEM to drive outcomes that support the secure and reliable supply of electricity in the WEM. 

What you will be doing 

Drive the development and present strategic comprehensive analysis to support a wide range of RCM functions and inform related decision-making processes.

Provide expert advice to internal and external stakeholders in formal/informal forums, providing insights into the technical, operational, and commercial aspects of the RCM, including risk assessments, drafting relevant material (memos, briefing notes, presentations, etc.) and recommendations to the management and senior leadership team in a timely manner.

Lead/undertake thorough reviews of technical documents, adhering to strict due-diligence, compliance with the regulatory instruments and confidentiality standards.

Identify, recommend and implement process improvements that minimise compliance risks, enhance operational efficiency, and strengthen organisational capabilities.

Lead the development of and maintain effective working relationships with external stakeholders that include retailers, generators, the network operator, and the policy maker, and support AEMO’s involvement in government policy initiatives and WEM regulatory matters. 

What you will bring to the role   

Understanding of the political and regulatory framework in which the energy industry operates, the operation of energy markets and power systems, and a comprehensive understanding of the changes in the energy sector and how these affect AEMO.

Demonstrated experience in delivering compliance while undertaking analysis, following procedures and developing reports in a commercial/regulatory environment.

Demonstrated ability to develop high quality written material and deliver effective and clear presentations which communicate highly technical, complicated concepts or issues to varied audiences.

Demonstrated experience successfully leading complicated technical and/or operational activities/projects by working effectively in large teams and across organisations.

Passion for energy with a strong attention to detail, a resilient and collegiate team member that actively coaches others in the team while being self-motivated and focussed.  

In return some of our benefits to you  

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more. 

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.  

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).  

About Our Process   

At AEMO we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome people of all backgrounds, including people with disabilities and neurodivergence and those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing us at tal•••@aemo.com.au

A high-growth B2B financial services technology company is appointing a Head of Quantitative in a newly created role to build and lead its next-generation modelling capability.

Operating at the intersection of wealth management, actuarial science and enterprise software, this business partners with tier-one financial institutions across Australia, the UK and Europe to modernise how financial advice is delivered at scale.

The Opportunity
The business is at a pivotal inflection point and is poised for continued market traction.

Having successfully deployed deterministic advice models at institutional scale, the next phase of growth requires the introduction of sophisticated stochastic modelling to unlock new markets, new products and materially improved customer outcomes.

As Head of Quantitative, you will:

Define the quantitative strategy


Lead the technical uplift to stochastic modelling


Build and scale a high-performing team


Act as the ultimate authority on model risk, integrity and regulatory alignment
This is a genuine player–coach role, with significant hands-on technical ownership.

What You’ll Be Responsible For
Architect and deliver stochastic modelling frameworks (Monte Carlo, scenario generation, multi-goal optimisation)


Lead the transition from deterministic Excel-based models to scalable calculation engines


Translate complex regulatory and advice policy requirements into robust quantitative logic


Build, mentor and scale a quantitative team across Australia and global markets


Partner closely with Product and Engineering to productionise models at scale


Engage directly with tier-one institutional clients, actuarial teams and regulators


Own quantitative governance, validation and model risk standards globally
What It Will Take to Make an Impact
Deep expertise in stochastic / actuarial / quantitative modelling

Experience operating in regulated financial services environments (wealth, retirement, insurance or advice technology)
